data.body:
Ported next-batch behind REBAR_COMPUTE (Tier C).

Impl: rebar._engine_support.next_batch (model/compute/render/CLI, 561 LOC) + next_batch_files (text->file-path heuristic, 127 LOC, split on its own seam). Reuses rebar.reducer/reduce_all_tickets + the shared list_states read path instead of subprocessing rebar show/list + an importlib reducer load. Library next_batch() + MCP now in-process under the switch (was the last subprocessing read).

Switch-in: dispatcher _compute_python bash helper (the Tier B _leaf_writes_python pattern), pinned to rebar._switch by a typo/case parity matrix.

Parity: dual-run byte-identical (stdout/stderr/exit, text + json) across REBAR_COMPUTE=bash|python for every deterministic scenario; the bash suite (test-ticket-next-batch-dispatcher.sh, 11 tests) passes under both. New gate tests/interfaces/test_next_batch_compute.py (23 cases).

Surfaced deviation (docs/bash-migration.md §5): bash's conflict_file diagnostic is non-deterministic (unordered set iteration under hash randomization); the port sorts it -> byte-stable, honoring the selector's 'deterministic' contract. Batch composition unaffected and still matches bash.

Status: default stays bash pending flip+soak. Interfaces tier 268/268 green.
